message:
chore(beads): record three closed bug findings (expr01, fuzz01, hll01)

Append three new closed issues to the beads issue log capturing
recently-resolved bug findings. The corresponding fixes are
already in the git history; this entry exists so beads triage,
bv label-health, and downstream `br ready`/`br show` users see
consistent status.

frankenpandas-expr01 (closed, P2):
- Multi-pass audit found that the fp-expr parser had no support
  for backslash escape sequences in string literals, so pandas
  eval/query strings like `"O\\'Brien"` would fail to parse,
  and multiple decimal points (`1.2.3`) weren't rejected until
  the trailing `parse::<f64>()` call failed deeper in the
  pipeline.
- Resolved by `fix(fp-expr): escape sequences + decimal
  validation` (commit 985fe07): added handling for `\n`, `\t`,
  `\r`, `\\`, `\'`, `\"` in string literals and an explicit
  too-many-decimals check, with six new tests pinning the
  behavior.

frankenpandas-fuzz01 (closed, P2):
- The `join_series` fuzz corpus only had 5 seeds, so the fuzzer
  was missing fast coverage of the join code's structural edges:
  empty series, duplicate labels, all-null values, null
  propagation, large overlap, disjoint labels, asymmetric
  cardinalities, cross product, mixed dtypes, and single
  elements.
- Resolved by `test(fuzz): 12 join_series edge-case seeds`
  (commit 06ab9fb): corpus extended to 17 seeds. Note:
  comparison harness for `JoinedSeries` uses `==` on payloads
  containing NaN, which causes false drift detection; the
  short-term workaround is to use `Null` instead of `NaN` in
  seeds.

frankenpandas-hll01 (closed, P2):
- `HyperLogLog::estimate()` used `2_u32.pow(r)` even though HLL
  registers can hold rho values up to 64 from `leading_zeros()`.
  For r > 31 this overflowed: panic in debug, silent wrap in
  release, producing wildly incorrect cardinality estimates on
  large or hash-skewed inputs.
- Resolved by `fix(fp-groupby): HyperLogLog integer overflow`
  (commit 2497512): switched to `2_f64.powi()` for the 2^r
  factor, added a test pinning estimate behavior at high rho,
  and verified all 66 fp-groupby tests still pass.

No code changes in this commit — beads metadata only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
